operatorAttributionText: Brought to you by Hereford BID
splashIntro:
Hello there. Yes, you. Come a little closer, I will not bite. My name is Richard, and a very long time ago, about seven hundred years ago, I drew a map. Not just any map. The biggest map of the whole world, and it is still here today, inside Hereford Cathedral.

Around the edges of my map, I drew some very strange creatures. Monsters. A man with ears as big as bedsheets. A bull that shoots fire. A man with one giant foot. Every one of them is real, because I drew them myself.

And here is my problem. They have escaped. Every single monster has climbed off my map and run away to hide all over Hereford. Naughty things.

That is where you come in. I need a brave monster hunter, and you look just right. Your job is to find all six. Look at the picture on your screen so you know what each monster looks like. Then keep your eyes wide open, because they like to hide in windows, in doorways, and sometimes right down low on the ground.

When you find a monster, tap the Caught it button, and I will pop it straight back onto my map. Find all six and you will be a proper monster hunter. Ready? The first one has the enormous ears.
tourCompleteNarration:
You did it. Every single monster, found and back on my map where it belongs. I knew you were the right hunter the moment I saw you.

Now for the best part. Walk back inside Hereford Cathedral and find the real map, the Mappa Mundi. It is enormous, it is seven hundred years old, and I drew every line of it myself. Get up close and look around the edges. You will find them all there, drawn in their proper places. The big ears, the fiery bull, the screaming plant, the lazy foot, the grumpy dragon and the slithery serpent. Every monster you caught today.

So the next time somebody tells you a map is boring, you can tell them that you once chased its monsters all around Hereford and put every single one of them back. Thank you, brave hunter. Richard of Haldingham, mapmaker, says farewell.
tourCompleteMessage: Every monster is back on the map. Now go inside the Cathedral and find them all on the real one.
tourCompleteSuggestion: Step inside Hereford Cathedral and find all six monsters on the giant 700 year old map.

stops.name: Church Street
stops.shortDescription: Find the monster with ears like bedsheets
stops.narration:
Well done, you made it to Church Street. This narrow little street is full of shops, and it is the perfect hiding place for a monster who loves to listen.

The monster here is called the Panotii. Look at your screen and you will see his ears. They are as big as bedsheets. At night he wraps himself up in them like a cosy blanket, and with ears that big he can hear every little secret on the street.

He is hiding somewhere very close to you right now. Have a good look around. Check the windows. Check the doorways. Look up high, and look down low. Can you spot those giant ears poking out?

When you find him, tap Caught it, and back onto the map he goes. Off you pop, hunter.
facts: The Panotii, drawn with ears large enough to sleep inside, appear on the real Mappa Mundi at the edge of the known world.
Church Street is one of Hereford's oldest lanes, lined with independent shops between the Cathedral and High Town.

stops.name: High Town
stops.shortDescription: Find the bull that fires its own poo
stops.narration:
Here you are in High Town, the big open square right in the middle of Hereford. Long ago, this is where people came to buy and sell their cows, so it is the perfect home for our next monster, because he is a bit like a bull.

He is called the Bonnacon. Look at the picture on your screen. He has a furry mane and curly horns. Now here is the funny part, and it is a little bit rude. When something chases the Bonnacon, it fires its own poo out of its bottom, splat, all over them. And the poo is so hot it burns. So whatever you do, do not chase this one. Just find him.

Look all around the square. Peek at the windows and the doorways, and check down low near the ground. Can you see the Bonnacon hiding?

When you find him, tap Caught it. And try not to stand behind him.
facts: Medieval bestiaries describe the Bonnacon defending itself by firing burning dung as it flees, said to cover three acres of ground.
Hereford cattle, with their distinctive white faces, are one of the most widely exported breeds in the world.

stops.name: The Old House
stops.shortDescription: Find the screaming plant with a grumpy face
stops.narration:
This grand black and white house is called the Old House, and it is very, very old. It was built more than four hundred years ago. Our next monster is hiding somewhere near it.

This one is not an animal at all. It is a plant. It is called the Mandrake, and it grows in the ground shaped like a tiny person, with little arms, little legs and a grumpy little face. Look at your screen and you will see him.

Here is the spooky part. If you pull a Mandrake out of the ground, it screams, so loudly that it could knock you off your feet. So we will not pull him. We will just find him.

Look all around the Old House. Try the windows, try the doorways, and look down low near the ground where a little root might hide. Can you spot his leafy head?

When you find him, tap Caught it, and on we go.
facts: The Old House was built in 1621 and is one of the last survivors of a row of timber-framed buildings that once filled High Town.
Legend held that a mandrake root screamed when pulled from the ground, so dogs were used to pull it up instead.

stops.name: All Saints Church
stops.shortDescription: Find the lazy monster with one giant foot
stops.narration:
Look up at this church. It is called All Saints. Can you see the tall pointy tower, the spire? For hundreds of years it leaned right over to one side, because long ago the builders stood it on top of an old buried rubbish pit. Clever people straightened it not so long ago, but for a very long time it looked like it might topple right over. Whoops.

The monster hiding here is the lazy one. He is called the Sciapod, and he has just one leg and one enormous foot. Look at your screen. He cannot really walk, so he lies on his back all day and holds his giant foot up in the air to use as a sunshade. Imagine being your very own umbrella.

He is hiding somewhere close by, probably having a nap. Look in the windows and the doorways, and check down low near the ground. Can you find that great big foot?

When you spot him, tap Caught it. He will not even wake up.
facts: The Sciapod is shown on the Mappa Mundi as a man lying on his back, using his single huge foot as a sunshade.
All Saints was begun around 1200, and for centuries its spire leaned because it was built over an old rubbish pit, until it was straightened not long ago.

stops.name: Castle Green
stops.shortDescription: Find the grumpy dragon with no castle to guard
stops.narration:
You have reached Castle Green, a lovely green park beside the river. It looks calm and peaceful now, but guess what once stood right here. A huge castle. One of the biggest in all of England. It is gone now, with only the grass left behind.

And that is bad news for our next monster, the Dragon. Look at your screen. Every dragon needs a castle to guard. But when this one turned up to do his guarding, somebody had to tell him the castle vanished hundreds of years ago. So now he just sits here feeling rather grumpy, puffing out little clouds of smoke.

Go and find him, and be kind. Look around the green, try the doorways nearby, and look down low to the ground. Can you spot the grumpy dragon?

When you find him, tap Caught it, and cheer him up a little.
facts: Hereford Castle was once one of the largest castles in England, but was dismantled over the centuries until almost nothing remained.
The column on Castle Green honours Admiral Lord Nelson, who was given the freedom of the city when he visited in 1802.

stops.name: Victoria Bridge
stops.shortDescription: Find the slithery sea serpent by the river
stops.narration:
Here is your very last monster, and the trickiest one of all. You are standing on Victoria Bridge, the white bridge that crosses the River Wye. Look down at the water.

The monster here is the Sea Serpent. Look at your screen. He is long and slithery, like a giant snake, with shiny eyes that glow like lanterns. Sailors used to say they saw him out at sea, as long as three boats lined up end to end.

He is hiding somewhere near the bridge. Have a really good look. Check the windows, check the doorways, and look right down low near the ground or the edge of the water. Can you find him?

When you spot the Sea Serpent, tap Caught it. That is all six. You clever thing. Now hurry back to the Cathedral, and I will meet you there.
facts: Victoria Bridge, the white suspension footbridge over the Wye, opened in 1898 in honour of Queen Victoria.
Medieval mapmakers filled the oceans of maps like the Mappa Mundi with sea serpents and monsters reported by sailors.

locationFacts.text: The Hereford Mappa Mundi is the largest medieval map of the world to survive, drawn on a single sheet of calf-skin vellum around the year 1300.
The map places Jerusalem at the very centre of the world, with east, not north, at the top.
Around its edges the map shows strange races and beasts, from dog-headed men to a bull that fights with fire.
The mapmaker signed his work as Richard of Haldingham and Lafford, which is why Richard is your guide on this hunt.
